title: betula alba subsp. excelsa
intro: Betula alba subsp. excelsa, commonly known as the silver birch, is a subspecies of the white birch tree, characterized by its striking white bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Betula alba subsp. excelsa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing skin irritations, as well as supporting the immune system and reducing stress. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Betula alba subsp. excelsa have included infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the bark in hot water or using the leaves and bark in a topical application.
